apache2.service.title: Apache HTTP Server
apache2.service.summary: Runs the Apache web server. It usually serves websites or reverse-proxies web applications on this machine.

apparmor.service.title: AppArmor profiles
apparmor.service.summary: Loads AppArmor security profiles. AppArmor can restrict what programs are allowed to access, even when those programs are running.

apt-daily.service.title: APT daily download task
apt-daily.service.summary: Runs Debian or Ubuntu package maintenance tasks, mainly checking for and downloading package metadata in the background.

apt-daily-upgrade.service.title: APT daily upgrade task
apt-daily-upgrade.service.summary: Runs package upgrade and cleanup maintenance tasks in the background. It is usually triggered by a timer.

autovt@.service.title: Automatic virtual terminal login prompt
autovt@.service.summary: Starts login prompts on virtual terminals when they are needed. Instances are created for terminal devices such as tty1.

console-getty.service.title: Console login prompt
console-getty.service.summary: Provides a login prompt on the main system console. It is mostly relevant for local or virtual machine console access.

console-setup.service.title: Console font and keymap setup
console-setup.service.summary: Applies console font and keyboard settings during boot. It normally exits after setup is complete.

container-getty@.service.title: Container login prompt
container-getty@.service.summary: Provides a login prompt for container consoles. It is mainly used on systems that run systemd inside containers.

cron.service.title: Cron scheduler
cron.service.summary: Runs scheduled jobs from crontab files. It is commonly used for recurring maintenance scripts and background tasks.

cryptdisks.service.title: Encrypted disk setup
cryptdisks.service.summary: Legacy Debian service for opening encrypted disks during boot. On many modern systems this is replaced by systemd cryptsetup units.

cryptdisks-early.service.title: Early encrypted disk setup
cryptdisks-early.service.summary: Legacy Debian service for opening encrypted disks early during boot, before normal filesystems and services start.

dbus.service.title: D-Bus system bus
dbus.service.summary: Runs the system message bus used by many desktop, system and management services to communicate with each other.

dbus-org.freedesktop.hostname1.service.title: Hostname service alias
dbus-org.freedesktop.hostname1.service.summary: Alias for the systemd service that handles hostname information over D-Bus.

dbus-org.freedesktop.locale1.service.title: Locale service alias
dbus-org.freedesktop.locale1.service.summary: Alias for the systemd service that handles locale and keyboard settings over D-Bus.

dbus-org.freedesktop.login1.service.title: Login manager alias
dbus-org.freedesktop.login1.service.summary: Alias for systemd-logind, which manages logins, seats, sessions and power-related user actions.

dbus-org.freedesktop.timedate1.service.title: Time and date service alias
dbus-org.freedesktop.timedate1.service.summary: Alias for the systemd service that handles time, date, timezone and NTP settings over D-Bus.

dbus-org.freedesktop.timesync1.service.title: Time synchronization alias
dbus-org.freedesktop.timesync1.service.summary: Alias for systemd-timesyncd, the lightweight network time synchronization service.

debug-shell.service.title: Debug root shell
debug-shell.service.summary: Can start an emergency root shell on tty9 for debugging. It is normally disabled because it is sensitive.

docker.service.title: Docker Engine
docker.service.summary: Runs the Docker daemon. Containers, images and Docker networks depend on this service.

dpkg-db-backup.service.title: dpkg database backup
dpkg-db-backup.service.summary: Creates backups of the Debian package database. This helps recover package state if the database becomes damaged.

e2scrub@.service.title: ext4 metadata scrub
e2scrub@.service.summary: Checks ext4 filesystem metadata for problems while the filesystem is online. Instances target specific filesystems.

e2scrub_all.service.title: ext4 metadata scrub for all filesystems
e2scrub_all.service.summary: Checks metadata for all eligible ext4 filesystems. It is usually started by a timer and exits when the check is complete.

e2scrub_fail@.service.title: ext4 scrub failure handler
e2scrub_fail@.service.summary: Handles failure reporting for an ext4 metadata scrub instance.

e2scrub_reap.service.title: ext4 scrub snapshot cleanup
e2scrub_reap.service.summary: Removes stale temporary snapshots that were used for online ext4 metadata checks.

emergency.service.title: Emergency shell
emergency.service.summary: Starts a very minimal emergency shell for serious boot or system recovery situations.

frp-gui.service.title: FRP Gui
frp-gui.service.summary: Runs the FRP Gui web interface project by Nisbo. It helps manage the local FRP client configuration without manually editing files over SSH.

frpc.service.title: FRP client
frpc.service.summary: Runs frpc, the FRP client. It connects this machine to an FRP server and exposes configured local services through tunnels.

fstrim.service.title: Filesystem trim
fstrim.service.summary: Tells SSDs and thin-provisioned storage which blocks are unused. This can help storage performance and space reclamation.

getty-static.service.title: Static login prompts
getty-static.service.summary: Starts fallback getty login prompts on tty2-tty6 when dbus and logind are not available.

getty@.service.title: Terminal login prompt
getty@.service.summary: Starts a login prompt on a text terminal such as tty1. This is what allows local console logins.

hwclock.service.title: Hardware clock compatibility service
hwclock.service.summary: Legacy service related to reading or writing the hardware clock. It is often masked on modern systemd systems.

ifup@.service.title: ifup network interface
ifup@.service.summary: Brings up a network interface using Debian ifupdown configuration from /etc/network/interfaces.

ifupdown-pre.service.title: ifupdown boot synchronization
ifupdown-pre.service.summary: Helper service used by Debian ifupdown networking during boot, before normal network interface startup.

ifupdown-wait-online.service.title: ifupdown wait online
ifupdown-wait-online.service.summary: Waits for ifupdown-managed network interfaces to become available before services that require the network start.

initrd-cleanup.service.title: initrd cleanup
initrd-cleanup.service.summary: Cleans up services and state from the initial RAM disk after the system switches to the real root filesystem.

initrd-parse-etc.service.title: initrd /etc parser
initrd-parse-etc.service.summary: Reads configuration from the real root filesystem while the system is still booting in the initial RAM disk.

initrd-switch-root.service.title: initrd switch root
initrd-switch-root.service.summary: Switches the system from the initial RAM disk to the real root filesystem during boot.

initrd-udevadm-cleanup-db.service.title: initrd udev cleanup
initrd-udevadm-cleanup-db.service.summary: Cleans temporary udev device database data after the system leaves the initial RAM disk.

keyboard-setup.service.title: Keyboard layout setup
keyboard-setup.service.summary: Applies the console keyboard layout during boot. It normally exits after setup is complete.

kmod.service.title: Kernel module helper alias
kmod.service.summary: Alias related to kernel module loading support.

kmod-static-nodes.service.title: Static kernel device nodes
kmod-static-nodes.service.summary: Creates the list of static device nodes requested by kernel modules.

logrotate.service.title: Log rotation
logrotate.service.summary: Rotates and compresses log files so they do not grow forever. It is usually triggered by a timer.

man-db.service.title: Manual page database
man-db.service.summary: Updates the man-page index database used by the man command. It is usually triggered by a timer.

mariadb.service.title: MariaDB database server
mariadb.service.summary: Runs the MariaDB database server. Websites and applications may use it to store persistent data.

modprobe@.service.title: Kernel module loader
modprobe@.service.summary: Loads a specific Linux kernel module. Instances such as modprobe@fuse.service load one module and then exit.

mysql.service.title: MySQL database server
mysql.service.summary: Runs the MySQL database server. Websites and applications may use it to store persistent data.

networking.service.title: Debian networking
networking.service.summary: Raises network interfaces using Debian ifupdown configuration. Stopping or restarting it can interrupt network access and SSH sessions.

nftables.service.title: nftables firewall
nftables.service.summary: Loads nftables firewall rules. If enabled, it controls packet filtering and network address translation.

nginx.service.title: nginx web server
nginx.service.summary: Runs nginx. It is commonly used as a web server, reverse proxy, TLS endpoint or gateway in front of apps such as Gunicorn services.

pam_namespace.service.title: PAM namespace setup
pam_namespace.service.summary: Sets up per-user or per-session filesystem namespaces when configured through PAM. Most simple servers do not use it directly.

postgresql.service.title: PostgreSQL database server
postgresql.service.summary: Runs PostgreSQL database clusters on Debian-style systems. Applications may depend on it for persistent data.

procps.service.title: Kernel parameter setup
procps.service.summary: Legacy alias related to applying kernel sysctl settings from procps configuration.

qemu-guest-agent.service.title: QEMU guest agent
qemu-guest-agent.service.summary: Lets a QEMU or Proxmox host communicate with the guest VM for actions such as shutdown, IP reporting and filesystem freeze.

quotaon.service.title: Filesystem quota activation
quotaon.service.summary: Enables disk quota tracking for filesystems that are configured to use quotas.

rc-local.service.title: rc.local compatibility
rc-local.service.summary: Runs /etc/rc.local for compatibility with older Linux startup setups. Modern systems usually prefer native systemd units.

rc.service.title: Legacy runlevel service
rc.service.summary: Compatibility service for older SysV init runlevel behavior. It is usually masked on systemd systems.

rcS.service.title: Legacy single-user boot service
rcS.service.summary: Compatibility service for older SysV init single-user boot behavior. It is usually masked on systemd systems.

rescue.service.title: Rescue shell
rescue.service.summary: Starts a minimal rescue environment for maintenance when normal multi-user boot is not possible.

serial-getty@.service.title: Serial console login prompt
serial-getty@.service.summary: Starts a login prompt on a serial console. This is common on virtual machines, servers and embedded systems.

snapd.service.title: Snap daemon
snapd.service.summary: Runs snapd, the service that manages Snap packages, refreshes and Snap application integration.

snapd.aa-prompt-listener.service.title: Snap AppArmor prompt listener
snapd.aa-prompt-listener.service.summary: Listens for userspace AppArmor prompt events used by snapd.

snapd.apparmor.service.title: Snap AppArmor profiles
snapd.apparmor.service.summary: Loads AppArmor security profiles generated and managed by snapd.

snapd.recovery-chooser-trigger.service.title: Snap recovery chooser trigger
snapd.recovery-chooser-trigger.service.summary: Snapd helper used on Ubuntu Core style systems to trigger recovery chooser behavior.

snapd.seeded.service.title: Snap seeding completion
snapd.seeded.service.summary: Waits until snapd has completed initial setup of preinstalled snaps.

ssh.service.title: OpenSSH server
ssh.service.summary: Runs the SSH server. It allows remote terminal logins and file transfers. Stopping it can disconnect administrators.

sshd.service.title: OpenSSH server
sshd.service.summary: Runs the SSH server. It allows remote terminal logins and file transfers. Stopping it can disconnect administrators.

system-update-cleanup.service.title: System update cleanup
system-update-cleanup.service.summary: Cleans up state after an offline system update.

systemd-ask-password-console.service.title: Console password requests
systemd-ask-password-console.service.summary: Displays system password requests on the console, for example when encrypted disks need a passphrase.

systemd-ask-password-wall.service.title: Wall password requests
systemd-ask-password-wall.service.summary: Forwards system password requests to logged-in users through wall-style messages.

systemd-backlight@.service.title: Backlight restore
systemd-backlight@.service.summary: Saves or restores display backlight brightness for a specific backlight device.

systemd-binfmt.service.title: Binary format setup
systemd-binfmt.service.summary: Registers additional executable binary formats with the kernel, such as interpreters for special file types.

systemd-boot-check-no-failures.service.title: Boot failure check
systemd-boot-check-no-failures.service.summary: Checks whether the previous boot completed without failures. It is part of systemd boot health tracking.

systemd-exit.service.title: Systemd exit helper
systemd-exit.service.summary: Internal systemd service used when a systemd instance exits.

systemd-firstboot.service.title: First boot setup
systemd-firstboot.service.summary: Initializes basic system settings on first boot, such as locale, hostname or machine ID, when needed.

systemd-fsck-root.service.title: Root filesystem check
systemd-fsck-root.service.summary: Checks the root filesystem for errors during boot when a check is required.

systemd-fsck@.service.title: Filesystem check
systemd-fsck@.service.summary: Checks a specific filesystem for errors during boot when a check is required.

systemd-fsckd.service.title: Filesystem check progress
systemd-fsckd.service.summary: Reports filesystem check progress while fsck is running.

systemd-gui.service.title: Systemd Gui
systemd-gui.service.summary: Runs this web interface through Gunicorn. Restart it after application updates so the new Python code is loaded.

systemd-halt.service.title: System halt
systemd-halt.service.summary: Internal service used by systemd when the machine is halted.

systemd-hibernate.service.title: System hibernate
systemd-hibernate.service.summary: Internal service used when the machine enters hibernation.

systemd-hibernate-resume@.service.title: Hibernate resume
systemd-hibernate-resume@.service.summary: Handles resume from hibernation for a specific swap or resume device.

systemd-hostnamed.service.title: Hostname manager
systemd-hostnamed.service.summary: Provides D-Bus APIs for reading and changing the system hostname and related machine information.

systemd-hybrid-sleep.service.title: Hybrid sleep
systemd-hybrid-sleep.service.summary: Internal service used when the machine enters hybrid sleep.

systemd-initctl.service.title: initctl compatibility daemon
systemd-initctl.service.summary: Provides compatibility for older tools that expect the SysV initctl interface.

systemd-journal-flush.service.title: Journal flush
systemd-journal-flush.service.summary: Moves early boot journal messages from temporary storage into persistent journal storage.

systemd-journald.service.title: systemd journal
systemd-journald.service.summary: Collects and stores logs for the system and services. journalctl reads from this service.

systemd-journald@.service.title: Namespaced systemd journal
systemd-journald@.service.summary: Runs a journal service instance for a specific journal namespace.

systemd-kexec.service.title: Kernel exec reboot
systemd-kexec.service.summary: Internal service used when rebooting directly into another kernel with kexec.

systemd-localed.service.title: Locale manager
systemd-localed.service.summary: Provides D-Bus APIs for locale and keyboard layout settings.

systemd-logind.service.title: Login manager
systemd-logind.service.summary: Manages user logins, sessions, seats, suspend and power button handling. Stopping it can disrupt logged-in users.

systemd-machine-id-commit.service.title: Machine ID commit
systemd-machine-id-commit.service.summary: Writes a transient machine ID permanently to disk when needed.

systemd-modules-load.service.title: Kernel module loading
systemd-modules-load.service.summary: Loads kernel modules configured for early boot.

systemd-network-generator.service.title: Network generator
systemd-network-generator.service.summary: Generates systemd-networkd configuration from kernel command line or other early boot inputs.

systemd-networkd.service.title: systemd-networkd
systemd-networkd.service.summary: Manages network interfaces using systemd-networkd configuration. Stopping it can interrupt network access.

systemd-networkd-wait-online.service.title: Wait for network online
systemd-networkd-wait-online.service.summary: Waits until systemd-networkd considers the network online before network-dependent services start.

systemd-networkd-wait-online@.service.title: Wait for network interface online
systemd-networkd-wait-online@.service.summary: Waits for a specific systemd-networkd-managed interface to become online.

systemd-pcrphase.service.title: TPM2 PCR phase marker
systemd-pcrphase.service.summary: Records boot phase measurements into TPM2 PCRs on systems that use TPM-based boot measurement.

systemd-pcrphase-initrd.service.title: TPM2 PCR initrd phase marker
systemd-pcrphase-initrd.service.summary: Records initrd boot phase measurements into TPM2 PCRs on systems that use TPM-based boot measurement.

systemd-pcrphase-sysinit.service.title: TPM2 PCR sysinit phase marker
systemd-pcrphase-sysinit.service.summary: Records early system initialization phase measurements into TPM2 PCRs.

systemd-poweroff.service.title: System poweroff
systemd-poweroff.service.summary: Internal service used by systemd when the machine powers off.

systemd-pstore.service.title: Persistent storage archival
systemd-pstore.service.summary: Archives platform persistent storage logs, such as firmware or kernel crash information, when available.

systemd-quotacheck.service.title: Filesystem quota check
systemd-quotacheck.service.summary: Checks filesystem quota information during boot when quotas are configured.

systemd-random-seed.service.title: Random seed
systemd-random-seed.service.summary: Loads and saves random seed data so the system has better entropy early during boot.

systemd-reboot.service.title: System reboot
systemd-reboot.service.summary: Internal service used by systemd when the machine reboots.

systemd-remount-fs.service.title: Root filesystem remount
systemd-remount-fs.service.summary: Remounts the root and kernel filesystems with their final mount options during boot.

systemd-repart.service.title: Disk repartitioning
systemd-repart.service.summary: Can repartition disks according to systemd-repart configuration. Usually inactive on simple servers.

systemd-rfkill.service.title: RF kill state restore
systemd-rfkill.service.summary: Saves and restores radio device block state, such as Wi-Fi or Bluetooth rfkill settings.

systemd-suspend.service.title: System suspend
systemd-suspend.service.summary: Internal service used when the machine enters suspend.

systemd-suspend-then-hibernate.service.title: Suspend then hibernate
systemd-suspend-then-hibernate.service.summary: Internal service used when the machine first suspends and later hibernates.

systemd-sysext.service.title: System extension merge
systemd-sysext.service.summary: Merges system extension images into /usr or /opt when systemd system extensions are used.

systemd-sysctl.service.title: Kernel variable setup
systemd-sysctl.service.summary: Applies kernel parameters from sysctl configuration files during boot.

systemd-sysusers.service.title: System user creation
systemd-sysusers.service.summary: Creates system users and groups declared by installed packages.

systemd-time-wait-sync.service.title: Wait for time synchronization
systemd-time-wait-sync.service.summary: Waits until system time has been synchronized before time-sensitive services continue.

systemd-timedated.service.title: Time and date manager
systemd-timedated.service.summary: Provides D-Bus APIs for changing time, date, timezone and network time settings.

systemd-timesyncd.service.title: Network time synchronization
systemd-timesyncd.service.summary: Keeps the system clock synchronized with network time servers. Accurate time is important for logs, TLS and scheduled tasks.

systemd-tmpfiles-clean.service.title: Temporary file cleanup
systemd-tmpfiles-clean.service.summary: Cleans old files from temporary directories according to tmpfiles configuration.

systemd-tmpfiles-setup.service.title: Temporary files setup
systemd-tmpfiles-setup.service.summary: Creates system files, directories and permissions declared in tmpfiles configuration.

systemd-tmpfiles-setup-dev.service.title: Device node setup
systemd-tmpfiles-setup-dev.service.summary: Creates static device nodes in /dev according to tmpfiles configuration.

systemd-udev-settle.service.title: Wait for udev
systemd-udev-settle.service.summary: Waits until udev has processed device events. It is mostly a compatibility helper for services that need settled devices.

systemd-udev-trigger.service.title: Trigger udev devices
systemd-udev-trigger.service.summary: Triggers udev coldplug events so devices discovered before udev started are processed.

systemd-udevd.service.title: udev device manager
systemd-udevd.service.summary: Manages device events and applies udev rules when hardware or virtual devices appear or change.

systemd-update-utmp.service.title: Boot and shutdown accounting
systemd-update-utmp.service.summary: Records system boot and shutdown events in utmp/wtmp accounting files.

systemd-update-utmp-runlevel.service.title: Runlevel accounting
systemd-update-utmp-runlevel.service.summary: Records runlevel changes in utmp/wtmp accounting files for compatibility with older tools.

systemd-user-sessions.service.title: User session permission
systemd-user-sessions.service.summary: Allows normal user logins after boot has reached the appropriate point.

systemd-volatile-root.service.title: Volatile root setup
systemd-volatile-root.service.summary: Sets up a volatile root filesystem when the system is configured to boot with a temporary writable root.

udev.service.title: udev alias
udev.service.summary: Alias for the udev device manager service, systemd-udevd.

user-runtime-dir@.service.title: User runtime directory
user-runtime-dir@.service.summary: Creates and manages /run/user/<uid>, the per-user runtime directory used by user services and desktop/session tools.

user@.service.title: User service manager
user@.service.summary: Runs a per-user systemd instance. It manages services and runtime state for one logged-in user.

x11-common.service.title: X11 compatibility service
x11-common.service.summary: Legacy Debian X11 setup service. It is often masked on servers without a graphical desktop.
